title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Classes Page
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Classes Page Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Challenge Classes Links: Laser Challenge Classes Laser Challenge Original Laser Challenge Pro Laser Challenge V2 Laser Challenge V2 ELS Laser Challenge Classes The idea to do this came from Scavenger Apple 's page, which does still exist, but has not been updated in a long long time. Basically, this is a list of different roles you can play on a team, using different type of Laser Challenge equipment. Laser Challenge Original top (46) Classes Here is a list of classes you can use with the original gear. It is recommended you do not mix V2 and Original LC gear due to some incompatibility. Laser Challenge Original Laser Challenge Pro top (15) Classes The Pro gear isn't that popular of the LC gear, but if you have the vests here are some classes for you. Laser Challenge Pro Laser Challenge V2 top (68) Classes The most popular of the LC gear. This has the widest variety (some duplicate) of classes. I would also include Radar and Gotcha gear in this section. Laser Challenge V2 Laser Challenge V2 ELS top (11) Classes Using the ELS system with the V2 gear you get a unique experience. There are a few team classes here. Laser Challenge V2 ELS

title: Khan Jal's Original Laser Challenge Classes Page
text: Khan Jal's Original Laser Challenge Classes Page Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Challenge Classes > Original Laser Challenge Classes Links: Original Laser Challenge Classes Assault Soldier Attack Spy Bag Soldier Body Guard Bomb Squad Bomber Bounty Hunter Captor Combat Medic Commander Commando Counter Sniper CyberWorks Laser Challengers Demolition Soldier Demolitions Specialist Decoy Soldier Doom Soldier Fragger General Guard/Sentry Heavy Weapons Soldier Infiltrator/Spy Information Relay Soldier The Insider Lunatic Officer Recon/Infiltration Operative Rifleman Scout Secret Soldier Sniper Sniper, S.E.A.L. Soldier Special Tactics Unit Squad Leader Standard Gunman/Soldier Standard Soldier Stealth Sniper Suicide Bomber Suicide Soldier Supreme 007 Tactical Ops Squad Techno Warrior Union Soldier Warlord Original Laser Challenge Classes All of these classes uses the Original Laser Challenge Gear. It does not use any of the newer stuff, but you can take these classes and make them to suit your league. Assault Soldier top Credit : Maniac Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol & E-XD Accessories : 1 Laser Trap & Holster for Pistol Info : Basic Assault Operative. They work best in groups of 1-2. Must be very stealthy. Attack Spy top Credit : Brian Caslis Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : None Info : Sneaks into an area with the regular spies in front of him/her and then attacks! Bag Soldier top Credit : Tommy Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : Any and every thing they can use, BAG Info : Has any weapons or accessories he/she can find or steal from the other team. When people loose their weapons, they come to the Bag Soldier and get new weapons. The Demolition Soldiers will use them a lot. Body Guard top Credit : Tony Schraeder Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol and EX-D Rifle Accessories : None Info : A Body Guard's job is to protect the Commander, with his/her life if it comes to it. He/she also supervises the Commander to see how good his plans are. Bomb Squad top Credit : Manta_X Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 Pistols Accessories : None Info : This is a way to get rid of Laser Traps and stinky players! If an enemy sets a trap... make the Bomb Squad pick it up and run with it! Although he/she may die, it's better than letting a more experienced player die! Bomber top Credit : Matt Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : Laser Traps Info : Uses Laser Traps and kills. Bounty Hunter top Credit : PhazerStorm Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 Modified Blasters and 1 Heavily Modified E-XD Accessories : 3-4 Laser Traps Info : The Blasters, E-XD, front, and back vests should be silenced and painted black. The bounty hunter runs into combat immediately when the game starts, and takes out as many people as possible. Captor top Credit : BornToBattle Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D Rifle Accessories : None Info : This person captures enemies, when recon is needed. They try not to kill the person, but wound them until they have 1 shot left. Combat Medic top Credit : Maniac Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : Optional Holster, Walkie Talkie Info : Combat Medics are the only team members that can reset vests. They should carry a walkie talkie so they can talk to other members that need to be revived. They can defend themselves with pistols. They are a great target because once they die, there is no one to revive people. Commander top Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D Rifle Accessories : Laser Trap Info : Your Commander is your leader. He/She makes the strategies and issues the orders. Protect him/her at will for he/she is valuable. Commando top Credit : Tiberium Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 EX-D Rifles Accessories : 3 Laser Traps Info : (Be sure to have something to keep the traps in). Ambushes snipers and demo solders. Counter Sniper top Credit : Chris Kelley Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D Accessories : None Info : Finds and destroys Snipers in teams of 2. CyberWorks Laser Challengers top Credit : BLAZE Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : A wide variety Accessories : A wide variety Info : A composed team of friends capable of overcoming any challenge. Captain: Founder, Can handle any challenge. 2Snippers: Excellent aiming. 2Killers: Quick, great tactics. 2Armorines: Can handle any challenge!!! and 1Savior: Trust me when I say the strongest challenger. Each member with the Super Comp Set and an extra EX-D. Also we have 1-3 Laser Traps. We are totally HARD CORE. Tell me what you think...the Captain: Rael(...BLAZE...) Demolition Soldier top Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : None Accessories : Laser Traps (as many as he/she can hold, at least 4) Info : Armed with only Laser Traps, the Demo Soldier should have an escort just in case he/she runs into trouble. His/her main job is to, before an assault begins, to strategically throw all of his/her Laser Traps into the enemy's base, hopefully confusing them and making them scatter. Demolitions Specialist top Credit : Maniac Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol w/holster Accessories : 2+ Laser Traps Info : Demolitions Specialists set and disarm bombs. They will most likely die, but they protect their team from bombs though. Decoy Soldier top Credit : Tommy Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : 1 Laser Trap Info : When battle starts the Decoy Soldiers run toward the other teams base. The enemy's will focus on them and your other Soldiers can attack. Doom Soldier top Credit : Blacklotus Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 Pistols ,2 EX-D Rifles and 1 Micro Blaster Accessories : 5 Laser Traps Info : His/her job is when his/her side is losing, to go and waste everyone he/she can. Fragger top Credit : Detonator Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol or Micro Blaster Accessories : Belt Info : A fragger is needed in capture the flag Laser Tag games. A Fragger NEEDS escort by at least 2 soldiers. The idea of the Fragger is to snatch the flag while the enemy is dealing with his/her escort(s), and to be able to run as fast as he/she can back to base safely with the flag tucked away in a belt around his/her waist. General top Credit : Maniac Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : Optional Holster Info : A General commands the teams forces. They make plans and execute orders. They should be protected well, a General only gets one life. Once he is dead, game over. He should run around and hide a lot, and he should look like all other players. Guard/Sentry top Credit : Tim Prince Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D Rifle Accessories : None Info : A Guards only job is protecting the main base, the Guard should stand/sit almost completely still, although if he/she hears leafs rustling or twigs snapping the Guard should get up and look around. It may seem kind of boring just standing around, but a Guard is a necessary. Heavy Weapons Soldier top Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Dual EX-D Rifles Accessories : None Info : Armed with not one but two EX-D's, a Heavy Weapons soldier has a duty to back-up the regular soldiers. Theoretically he/she could fire in two directions at once maybe giving enough cover to the regular soldiers if they needed to retreat. Infiltrator/Spy top Equipment : Micro Gun Vest Weapon(s) : Micro Gun Accessories : Walkie-Talkie Info : The Infiltrator/Spy should keep an eye on the enemy base most if not all of the time. He/She should be very well hidden so they do not see him/her. As soon as he/she sees them getting ready to attack or do something else that might be considered important enough, he/she should contact the Info-Relay Soldier, and he/she should warn the Commander. Information Relay Soldier top Credit : Tommy Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : Walkie-Talkie Info : These Soldiers get information from the Spy(s) and relay it to the Commander. The Insider top Credit : Ricky Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol and Micro Gun Accessories : None Info : The Insider will hide the Micro Gun in his/her pocket and then surrender. While the Insider isn't under surveillance he/she will pull out the Micro Gun and get as many shots on anybody that's around or shoot before they plan to kill him/her. Lunatic top Credit : Alex Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Anything that they can carry Accessories : As many Laser Traps as possible Info : This is probably the funniest class. You are a lone shark. All you do is go after both teams with your bombs and guns, or can make an alliance with one and demolish one. Fun heh? Officer top Credit : The Grove Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : This person is the next rank lower to the Commander. He/She is needed for bigger games with many players. His/Her job is to be like the Commander, only he/she should be with a different group than the Commander. You should give the rifles to the Soldiers, not the Officers. Also if your team is about ready to lose, then you can have the Officer be a back-up soldier. Recon/Infiltration Operative top Credit : Maniac Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol & Micro Blaster Accessories : None Info : Recon Operatives are meant to attack the base with some cover, to make the operation look normal. Once they get hit plenty, they surrender. The guards will take his pistol, and his micro blaster should be hidden in a large pocket. He should listen in and get info on the enemy. Once he isn't under watch, he should escape, using the micro blaster if he has to. Then he reports back to base. Skilled Recon specialists are usually successful, unless they enemy searches the person and takes their micro blaster. Rifleman top Credit : The Grove Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D Rifle Accessories : Heavy Camouflage, and 1 or 2 Laser Traps Info : This person is mainly needed for capture the flag games. His job is to sneak around in the woods and look for the flag or the enemy base. Once he finds it he lay the laser traps where the enemy base is. He also can be a main assault trooper. Scout top Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : None Info : The Scout's job is to patrol along your bases perimeter making sure everything is ok. Secret Soldier top Credit : Blacklotus Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Modified Pistol and Pistol Accessories : Walkie Talkie ,Laser Trap, and whatever Info : The modified pistol has a Micromax blaster 1/5 the size, a Laser Pointer, and Hunting Scope. They hide until told to move in. Until the other side thinks that your team is losing, and you are out of people, come out of hiding and try to kill every one on the other team still alive. Sniper top Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D Rifle (with foam taped over the speaker slots to deafen the sound) Accessories : None Info : The Sniper's mission is to attack the enemy base from a great distance. With his/her weapon's sound lower than normal, the enemy might not know where it is coming from. Normally an attack by the Sniper shouldn't start until either side is starting to move. Here is a great Instruction Guide made by Nic Bertino . Sniper, S.E.A.L. top Credit : Nic Bertino Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : E-XD Accessories : Grappling Hook, Foot Spikes, Hand Claws, Tree Stands Info : A Sniper (a good one) should stay alone ALWAYS. A Sniper should be your BEST player, and have great marksmanship. Acting out of hiding, a sniper should be quick and stealthy. Proper tactics MUST be used in order for a Sniper to be effective. Here are the Sniper rules: 1. Don't get caught. 2. Don't get caught. 3. Don't get CAUGHT! Soldier top Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D Rifle Accessories : None Info : Of all the classes, the Soldiers should be the most common. The Soldiers are a team's main army. They are given orders from the Commander and should follow them without question. Special Tactic Unit top Credit : Xtreme Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D (modified), Poppers Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : This is a unit that goes into the enemy's base without being noticed. The gun must be silenced. They are the best of the best. The poppers are for a distraction, while they find the flag. Squad Leader top Credit : Capt.Moore Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D blaster, (Laser Pistol at his/her side) Accessories : 1 Laser Trap Info : Squad Leader controls 3 soldiers. He/she is rank under the First Officer (Commander, First officer, Squad leader). Squad leader take orders from them. For instance if the commander wanted to attack from two sides, this is where the Squad Leader would come in. Standard Gunman/Soldier top Credit : Maniac Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol Accessories : Optional Holster Info : The name says it. These guys are the rookies. They work best in teams of 2-4. Stealth Sniper top Credit : Maniac Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : E-XD Modified, explained below Accessories : Binoculars (optional), cammo clothes Info : A Stealth Sniper is a stealthy sniper. They must move quickly and quietly. Their E-XD should be modified with a mute button and extra range attachments. E-mail on how to make these mods. They sniper from long range and should have a lot of cammo on. A good spot for them is in a tree or in a ditch. Suicide Bomber top Credit : Jabberwocky Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D Rifle Accessories : 1 Laser Trap, Death Wish (Figuratively) Info : This one carries the Demolition Soldier one step further... a Demo Soldier that PERSONALLY ENSURES HIS/HER BOMB HITS PEOPLE! Tripping his/her bomb and running into crowds, he/she does some damage and does it better because there is a very slim margin for failure. Suicide Soldier top Credit : Jabberwocky Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : EX-D Rifle Accessories : 1-2 Laser Traps Info : Runs into the enemy base the second the game starts and shoots as many people as possible. Takes down the enemy for the sane people. THEIR MISSION PROFILE IS TO DIE! Sometimes they win the game. Supreme 007 top Credit : Bond, James Bond Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Incredibly modified EX-D Rifle Accessories : None Info : The person does what ever they want because they have the best gun. The gun includes: night vision scope, laser pointer, two normal pistols reduced to 1/10 of size, magnified beams, flashlight, all black gun, no reloading required, no lights or sound, and 90 degree reflector attachment. This gun is real. I did not make it up because I own it. Tactical Ops Squad top Credit : Maniac Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Varies for Assault, Demo and Recon, read below Accessories : Varies for Assault, Demo and Recon, read below Info : The name explains them. Tactical Ops Teams contain of Assault experts, Demo (Demolitions) experts, and Recon/Infiltration. Assault members take: 1 E-XD, 1 Pistol w/ holster, & 1 Laser Trap. They do frontal attacks and sneak up attacks. They should be stealthy. They work in groups of 2-4. They also attack snipers. Demo members should take: 1 Ultrawide w/ strap, 2-4 Laser Traps, and a backpack to carry Laser Traps in. Their job is to attack large groups of people plus arm & disarm Laser Traps. They work best in a group of 2. Recon members should have: 1 Micro blaster. Hide it in a pocket. Recon members gather enemy info of any type. They should be VERY stealthy. They also move in after Assault or Demo teams and pick up any lost weapons. They work best in split up groups of 2. Techno Warrior top Credit : roverT* Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol "modified" Accessories : None Info : A Techno Warrior is devoted to the game and will use any fair and true way to win. With a series of modifications on his gun, "such as a laser rifle modified to fit inside of the already existing gun" and remotes on the sides of the gun. He/She will always strive to fight back and win. Union Soldier top Credit : ddc007 Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Pistol and EX-D Rifle Accessories : 2 Laser Traps Info : They guard the person on their team who gets the flag. Warlord top Credit : PhazerStorm Equipment : Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 1 Super Modified E-XD Accessories : None Info : This person should be a very experienced player, he/she is in control of the entire operation and command of all troops.

title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Pro Classes Page
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Pro Classes Page Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Challenge Classes > Laser Challenge Pro Classes Links: Laser Challenge Pro Classes Ambush Squad Assassin Combat Medic Commander Defense Soldier Destroyer Soldier Drone Soldier Heavy Weapons Soldier Infiltrator/Spy Sniper Sniper or Sniper Squad Sniper Sneaker Soldier Special Forces Operative Trap Layer Laser Challenge Pro Classes All of these classes use the Laser Challenge Pro Gear. To get to most of these classes a certain trick is going to have to be used. For example: if you notice that the Combat Medic has a total of 10 remove-a-hits. Now normally that is impossible, because you only get one. Well not if a person who has another vest keeps turning his off, then on, enters a mode, and transfer's his/her only remove-a-hit to the Combat Medic. Then he repeats the procedure by turning it back off, then on, ... you get it now? Good. Because at least 3 of these classes use it (not only a remove-a-hit, but also Max Blasts and Shields). Ambush Squad top Credit : Shawn Game Mode : Rapid Fire Mode, Unlimited Ammo - 2+3+e Accessories : 10 Max Blast Info : This class usually consists of 1-2 units with 3-4 men/women in each. Each unit should have a commander, a sniper, and 1-2 assault solders. This class must be very stealthy and use guerrilla warfare and their fire power to ambush opponents that may wonder too far away from their comrades. This class can also be used to ambush enemy bases. Assassin top Credit : Shawn Game Mode : Silent Mode - 1+2+e Accessories : 10 Max Blast Info : The Assassin must be very skilful and stealthy. The purpose of the Assassin is for him/her to sneak up on an opponent (preferably the opponent's most skilled player) and sniper them with one of their Max Blast to try to put them out of the game, without your opponent knowing what occurred. Combat Medic top Game Mode : Unlimited Rapid Fire Mode - 2+2+e Accessories : 10 Remove a hits Info : The Combat Medic is supplied with 10 Remove a hits. If necessary he/she may use one, if necessary. The Combat Medic's main job is to lend his/her remove a hits to his/her teammate's who need them. Commander top Game Mode : Pro Mode - 2+e Accessories : Laser Trap Info : Your Commander has less hits than most people on your team, because he/she should be the best on your team. He/she should transfer all of his/her max blasts to the Heavy Weapons Soldier. Defense Soldier top Game Mode : Double Shield Mode - 3+1+e Accessories : 25 Shields Info : The Defense Soldier's main task is to keep everybody equipped with at least 2 shields. Everybody should try and only use the 10 sec shield only. Destroyer Soldier top Credit : Shadow Stalker Game Mode : Unlimited Rapid Fire- 2+3+e Accessories : 3 Laser Traps, 30 Max Blasts, and 5 Shields Info : There should be just 2 or 3 of these guys on the team because even 2 would be powerful enough to destroy most of the other team. I would have these guys be multi purpose soldiers. At first, they are Snipers, then, when the battle gets going good, or bad as the case may be, the Destroyer Soldier comes in and attacks the base. Drone Soldier top Credit : Pheniox Game Mode : Rapid Fire Mode, Unlimited Ammo - 2+3+e Accessories : None Info : The Drone Soldier ambushes the enemy base by using his/her 6 second auto fire's. When he/she uses up his/her auto fire's, they become a regular soldier. Heavy Weapons Soldier top Game Mode : War Mode - 3+3+e Accessories : 30 Max Blasts Info : The Heavy Weapons Soldier, armed with 30 Max Blasts, should back up the regular soldiers. He should use only his max blasts, aiming carefully to make sure he has a good shot. Infiltrator/Spy top Game Mode : Lights Out Mode - 2+1+e Accessories : 10 Shields Info : The Spy sneaks right up to the enemy base and watches them very carefully. If the Spy sees anything out of the ordinary, he/she reports it immediately to the nearest Soldier. Sniper top Game Mode : Silent Mode - 1+2+e Accessories : 10 Shields Info : The Sniper finds a nice comfy spot, looks around at the scenery, then starts opening fire at any opposing team member he/she can hit. With no sounds, it is hard to tell where a Sniper is (providing he/she has good cover). A Sniper must still be careful for the lights on his gun still work, and when you type in a code it makes the code sounds. Here is a great Instruction Guide made by Nic Bertino . Sniper or Sniper Squad top Credit : GreedyMan Game Mode : Silent Mode - 1+2+e Accessories : Cammo, Laser Traps, smoke bombs, Flash bangs Info : In a basic sniper scenario, you have one teammate on the highest point of your base (we play at a playground an night) his gun is usually modified so the motion maker, light emitter, and possibly the speaker are disconnected. Not only does this save battery power but it helps with stealth. Strap a laser pointer on it, to add to the effect (binnoculars help). Have him/her sit at the top and look for anything that moves. He/she can't be seen, hiding is second to a clear shot. Also it helps to equip your team with walkie-talkies as to make sure he doesn't shoot you (at 300 feet we all look the same) and to make sure no one sneaks up on him. A Sniper Team consists of two snipers, silenced, in cammo, with an armed compliment of grunts, usually 3-5, and as many Laser Traps as possible. The Snipers can pick off any contacts while there still too far away to know who's killing 'em. and the grunts help in close combat. Have planned reactions to situations, if you all freeze when a Laser Trap rolls your way, you're toast. This is an effective sweep-up-squad. Plus if you're charging a base, you can easily divide up into smaller teams or pairs to surround an enemy. NOTE: Just and idea, all walkie-talkies work on the same freq. (unless you can redo the circuit board, and tune several into the same number). So if you can use CB's or the Motorola 20 channel or similar ones, it's better, but the old failsafe, used since the revolution. Hand signals and code words. Useful party check is yell "THUNDER" and they should respond "CLASH". Simple but effective. Sniper Sneaker top Credit : Shawn Game Mode : Lights Out Mode - 2+1+e Accessories : 10 Max Blast Info : The purpose of this class is just what its name means. They carefully and silently stakeout and destroy their opponents deadly snipers. Soldier top Game Mode : Rapid Fire Mode, Unlimited Ammo - 2+3+e Accessories : None Info : The Soldier is the standard class. Equipped with unlimited ammo, the should be positioned to do be most effective. Special Forces Operative top Credit : kravus Game Mode : Unlimited Rapid Fire 2+3+e Accessories : 4 max blasts, 5 hit removers, 6 shields Info : The Special Forces Operative sweeps across the enemy base while finding the weakest point in their defenses. They sneak in and take out as many men/women as possible while staying hidden. Trap Layer top Credit : Ben Turk Game Mode : Lights Out Mode - 2+1+e Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : This class would work in teams of two, and would be in constant contact with each other. One would remain hidden somewhere where they could see any incoming attackers. If any attackers are spotted coming toward the base, he/she would contact the other, who would set some laser traps on long timer setting, and then retreat to a safe distance to snipe. When the attackers got to the point where the laser traps were, they would (Hopefully!) go off, at which point the sniping Trap Layer would open fire, and the other team member would come in from behind and cut them off. This tactic would work well in a forest game, where the traps would be harder to spot.

title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge V2 Classes Page
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge V2 Classes Page Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Challenge Classes > Laser Challenge V2 Classes Links: Laser Challenge V2 Classes 009 Alpha: Invisible Snipers Assassin Assault Trooper Basic Infantry Battlekat Squadron Alpha Battlekat Squadron Beta Bodyguard Captain Captain Cavalry Combat Medic Commander Commander Hostage Trooper Commando Communications Soldier Cover Sniper Covert Operative Demolition Soldier Distractor Expendable Squad Flag Guard General General Command Ground/Battle Field Turret Operator Guard Guerilla Commandos Guerilla Resistance Heavy Medic Heavy Weapons Grunt Heavy Weapons Guy Heavy Weapons Soldier Infantry Infiltrator/Spy Last Resort Sniper Lone Sniper Medic Night Team Member Nocturnal Super Soldier Personal Soldier Psycho Ranger Reaper Squad Recon Royal Bomb Squad Scout Seal Team Six Secret Commander Sharp-Shooter Sniper Sniper Commander Sniper Director Sniper Rendition Sniper Squad Soldier Spy Stealth Ninja Super Soldier Support Troops Suicide Soldier Super Sniper Team Pet Terminator Trickster Turret Operator Urban Shock Trooper (UST) V2 Slaughters Weapons Handler Laser Challenge V2 Classes These classes use the Laser Challenge V2 equipment. Please note: Laser Challenge: Laser Traps RESET the V2 vests 80% of the time. It is NOT recommended you use them with V2 gear! 009 top Credit : Daniel Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 Pistols, 2 Ultrawides, 2 Firestorms Accessories : Backpack and Walkie Talkie Info : This class has to be very skillful. He/she should be one of the teams best player, and needs lots of equipment. Alpha: Invisible Snipers top Credit : Jack da Ripper Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorms (silent) Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : This class is designed never to be seen. All Snipers must be at least 500 feet away and always out of sight of the enemy. The Snipers rarely come in contact with each other and only use the walkie talkies when a hit has been recorded. Assassin top Credit : 20belowzero Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 1 Firestorm, 1 Ultrawide Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : The Assassin should set up in a perfect sniping position to kill the opponent. He/she shouldn't be seen and works well with a Spy. Assault Trooper top Credit : Deucey Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 Ultrawides Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : Takes assignments from Spy or Commander, and does the job. Most common troop seen by enemy commando groups. They come in and finish the enemy one by one before they can regroup. Use maxblasts at this time for quick kills. Basic Infantry top Credit : Chuck Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 V2 Pistols and an Ultrawide, or a Firestorm Accessories : None Info : Basic Infantry are the grunts of an army. They are lightly armed, are very mobile, and capable of trapping enemies with Ultrawide or Pistol fire. While members with Firestorms blast away from long distances for the kill, they can carry a backpack if more than 2 weapons are used. When using Ultrawide and Pistol they may only used one at a time, not both. When carrying a Firestorm, they can't use a Pistol. If using Ultrawide and Pistol, and they want a second Pistol, then they may get it. If 2 Pistols, then they both may be used at once. Battlekat Squadron Alpha top Credit : Narayan Boston Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Varies for each group member. Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : The Battlekat Squadron Alpha Class is the class of soldiers that mixes precision and rapid fire with volume. They work separate from each other very often but their mission objectives make them a high-powered team. They communicate with each for best results on their missions. Quantum Fireball is the Most Dangerous Soldier (MDS) on the squad. He carries dual firestorms and dual V2 Pistols. Commander Battlekat (that's me) carries a Firestorm, an Ultrawide, and dual V2 Pistols. Everyone carries a walkie talkie. Battlekat Squadron Beta top Credit : Nitro Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Varies for each group member. Accessories : Varies for each group member. Info : The members of the Battlekat Squadron Beta (Beta for short), are there to back-up Battlekat Squadron Alpha. If a mission fails, in comes Beta to clean up the mess. The members should be experts in covert ops (operations which require stealth), and search, and rescue. Body Guard top Credit : Nitro Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 V2 Pistols, Firestorm Accessories : None Info : Bodyguards protect and take hits for VIPs (Very Important Person). Captain top Credit : Nitro Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 V2 Pistols, Firestorm Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : The Captain basically commands squadrons of soldiers or escorts VIPs (Very Important Person). Captain top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Ultrawide Rifle Accessories : None Info : He should be a good backup for the Commander. Cavalry top Credit : Evan Ostermann Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm, Ultrawide, Pistol Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : When everyone else on his/her team is dead, he/she comes in and KILLS. Combat Medic top Credit : Deku Mask Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Dual V2 Pistols Accessories : None Info : The Combat Medic carries two V2 pistols in his/her hands during the game. The V2 pistol is used to reset team mates who have been killed. (The Combat Medic's team mates can be reset a MAXIMUM of 3 times). The V2 pistol in the Combat Medics other hand is used for self-defense and attack on the other team while he/she tries to get to a fallen team mate. (Note: A player who is dead cannot move and the Combat Medic must find them). Commander top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm Accessories : None Info : The Commander is always armed with the most powerful weapon. He/she should be good at making strategies and plans. The team should try to protect their Commander the best that they can. Commander Hostage Trooper top Credit : Brady Lee Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm (Preferably) Accessories : None Info : As soon as the game starts, the "Commander Hostage Trooper" runs into the enemy base and try's to take the Commander hostage and demands a ransom of whatever he/she wants. I've done this and I think it's a good idea, it's amazing the kind of ransom's you can come up with. Commando top Credit : Nitro Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol, Firestorm Accessories : None Info : Commandos should be stealthy. They are to run special missions such as sabotage and capturing enemy commanders. Commandos should work in groups of 1-3. Communications Officer top Credit : BSeltzer Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : Numerous Walkie Talkies Info : This officer does little more than secondary guarding of the base and communicating with players in the field. He/she should have connection with all players in the field for his/her side. This means basically, he/she has a ton of walkie talkies, each connected to only one player, or he/she has one walkie talkie and all field players have walkie talkies connected to the exact same frequency. Using a 14 ch., 38 sub ch. radio (purchased at Wal-Mart for about $25 a piece), every player can communicate in sync with up to 2 mi. separating each player. This is by far the most effective way to know where each player is. Back to the job of the player, he/she can alert the commander if troops are in trouble, who can send backup when necessary. Cover Sniper top Credit : 20belowzero Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 1 Firestorm Accessories : None Info : This Cover Sniper should only be used in a few type of teams. This is because the Cover Sniper sits in one spot the whole game and snipes. If the person is very stealthy, the opponent shouldn't know where he/she is. Covert Operative top Credit : Dan Procacci Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : Walkie Talkie, Laser Pointer, and Black Clothes Info : Very similar to the night Team, the Covert Operative is a scout, spy, and sniper. He/she uses stealth and has a pretty good knowledge of the terrain and the enemy they are fighting. He/she has a walkie talkie (preferably the Motorola ones with 14 channels and 32 interference codes) so he can keep in contact with the main force. It helps to be wearing a black ski mask and ski goggles. (Ever hear of the computer game Rainbow Six?) It's also a good idea to wire in an earpiece so you won't make any unnecessary noise. When I play, my whole three man team consists of Cover Operatives. We can usually take down six guys in stealth in about 2.5 minutes. The player needs to be very fast. Demolition Soldier top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Ultrawide Accessories : None Info : This soldier, armed with an Ultrawide, is to use Max Blasts on the enemy's base constantly. He/she should be an expert at turning the gun on and off quickly to get max blasts. Distractor top Credit : John Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Dual V2 Pistols with black tape over the lights Accessories : Walkie Talkie and Mini laser/remote control Info : The Distractor's main objective is to get the infantry of the enemy to go after him as the soldiers from his/her group rushes their base. Expendable Squad top Credit : Insomniac Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Ultrawide, V2 Pistol Accessories : T.V. remote, Walkie Talkie Info : The expendable squad was meant to help the Nocturnal Super Soldiers and as the name suggests, they are considered expendable. The Expendable Squad is supposed to act as a distraction while the Nocturnal Super Soldiers kick ^&*. They are also supposed to gather enemy locations for the Nocturnal Super Soldiers, beyond that they are free to kill the %^&^&*&!!!! Flag Guard top Credit : BornToBattle Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : None Info : This class guards the flag in capture the flag, and cannot surrender under any circumstances, because he/she is the teams only hope. Should be very tactical when it comes to cover. General top Credit : Nitro Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm, V2 pistol, and TV remote Accessories : None Info : The General commands 90% of the troops. The only troops he\she doesn't control are the ones who stay inside the base. General Command top Credit : Anthony Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Dual Pistols Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : Should be a tactical expert. He/she leads forces into enemy lines. Ground/Battle Field Turret Operator top Credit : Steve Walsh Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm w/ Tripod, V2 Pistol Accessories : None Info : The Field Turret Operator operates a turret on the battle field. The Operator may want to cover up in a neat bush or shrub to hid from enemies. If it comes to a point where the enemies get to close, the Operator uses his/her gun to chase them away. Guard top Credit : Minith Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : None Info : The role of this person is to guard the base. They are equipped with an Ultrawide Rifle making them capable of taking down small groups of enemy soldiers. They should be prepared to fight against impossible odds. Guerilla Commandos top Credit : Mike Hansen Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Two V2 Pistols, One Ultrawide Accessories : Camo Info : Guerilla Commandos are a team's secret weapon. They need to be silent, hide well in trees/tall grass/weeds, and deathly accurate. They hide among the foliage, quickly creeping along, and shooting anything they see to pieces with their two pistols. If they by-chance come across a patrol or a well guarded area of enemy territory, that's when the Ultrawide comes in handy. Be as quiet as you can. No one knowing your position is one of your best tools. Guerilla Commandos have little use in Urban environments, so just choose another class when street-fighting. Guerilla Resistance top Credit : The DK Hitman Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 3 Members, 1 Firestorm, 2 Ultrawides, 1 Pistol per person Accessories : Walkie Talkies Info : This is a mobile sniper/attack unit. They deploy together and stay as a group unless they deploy the sniper and leave him behind. The sniper gets the Firestorm and provides fire support with max blasts, if he has them. He should stay out of the fray at least 150 feet, and get high if possible. The other two teammates should be fast little guys so they can run quickly and for a long time without tiring. The sniper should coordinate the attack and warn if enemy reinforcements are on the way. The sniper can fight a prolonged engagement and the fighters can fall back to protect him. Good for patrolling controlled territory around a base to act as interceptors for enemy attack squads, and to make hit and run attacks on fortified enemies. Heavy Medic top Credit : Rusty Sergeant Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm, Ultrawide, V2 Pistol Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : This class can defend itself and the person he/she is resetting. He/she can clean the area of enemies, then he/she can safely reset his target. Heavy Weapons Grunt top Credit : 20belowzero Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 1 Firestorm, 1 Ultrawide Accessories : None Info : The Heavy Weapons Grunt carries the Ultrawide in his/her left hand and a Firestorm in the right. The Ultrawide should be on wide blast mode. He/she can go trigger happy with the firestorm while swiping a huge shot every five seconds from his gun. Heavy Weapons Guy top Credit : The Phoenix Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Dual Pistols, Dual Ultrawide, Firestorm Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : TOTAL CARNAGE! A complete nutter loaded to the teeth with destruction, he/she is not allowed out until the 30 second "ready" signal. He/she will need the max blasts and blast shields. If he/she can't carry the weapons, he/she can take as much as he/she can carry (has belt holsters for the pistols). Heavy Weapons Soldier top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Dual Firestorms Accessories : None Info : Armed with two Firestorms, the Heavy Weapons Soldier's job is to attack soldiers with his/her weapons until they are dead. He/she can also provide cover and back up for regular or special soldiers. Infantry top Credit : Cory Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol and 2 Ultrawides Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : Infantry is the main artillery. It's mainly used as back up for the main troops. Infiltrator/Spy top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : Armed with only a pistol the Infiltrator/Spy is to keep a strong eye on the enemy's base. The report to the Commander. Last Resort Sniper top Credit : Nothing Man Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Ultrawide or anything silenced. Accessories : Dark clothes, preferably with a hood Info : This class should have small people. This is really only good for all out wars, when everyone dies. This LRS hides in a good hiding spot, and when there is only one other person left, they cover him/her. If the other person dies they just hide and stay there. Then they can keep a game going as long as possible. Lone Sniper top Credit : Chuck Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm, V2 Pistol Accessories : Cammo and Walkie Talkie Info : The Lone Sniper is responsible for guarding your base, and eliminating individual players, or squads. They use the insane firepower of the Firestorm to destroy their target. Medic top Credit : Tim Prince Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : None Info : The medic's job is to seek out team mates who have been killed and can reset soldiers or anyone else 2 times, that means 3 lives. The Commander can only be reset once. Also, the medic cannot fire or even load the pistol. If he/she is caught doing so the person is automatically dead. If there is a medic on a team that means that a team member cannot move after he/she is dead, the medic must find them. Night Team Member top Credit : Greg Vandagriff Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 1 V2 Pistol as well as others depending on position Accessories : Varies from class to class, see below Info : The Night Team was created by my laser challenge group. The Night Team was the group of people that everyone wanted to stay away from, simply because of their ability to drop a person quickly and swiftly, and most importantly, silently. A Night Team ideally operates during night games, as this is when they are most effective. Every Night Team member operating at night should be wearing black or camo, for obvious reasons. Each member should also ideally be quiet, soft soled shoes, like moccasins, while they look silly, they are verrrrry quiet. To communicate with each other, we developed hand signals, identical to the SWAT teams. Many specific positions are available on a night team, usually only 3-4 people are in each Night Team, too many people equals too much noise. The positions are as follow: Commander - V2 Pistol, Walkie Talkie, flashlight, binoculars (optional), backpack (Also optional). This is the only unit on a team that should carry a walkie talkie, otherwise too much noise will result. Scout - V2 Pistol, those little white fireworks that you throw on the ground, I think they're called poppers... flashlight, a small amount of rope for setting traps or for climbing fences, a backpack (optional). This unit generally travels 50-75 feet ahead of the rest of the team, this job is creepy at night since a lot of times you're all by yourself in enemy territory, although this job is very possibly the most important of them all. Suppression Unit - 1 Ultrawide, 1 V2 for backup, flashlight. This is the basic combat unit of the group, generally these should be the most versatile players in the group, they should be able to take out 2 or 3 other players for every one suppression unit lost. Just think "Doom" or "Quake" And pretend your shootin' up monsters and you have an adrenaline rush, you should do just fine. Sniper- 1 Firestorm. The sniper is a very nerve racking role, you have to be calm, controlled, and be ready to take that ever important shot.... and hit. Usually operates separate from the group, this is my favorite position, a good strategy is to make a mini sniper base where you can remain hidden throughout the game and snipe from, high points are nice, but they are also the first place the enemy looks for a sniper. Nocturnal Super Soldier top Credit : Insomniac Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm, Ultrawide, V2 pistol, and Stealth Atomizer Accessories : Walkie Talkie, Binoculars, and Camo Info : I invented this class to be the ultimate night time weapon. Each member should be armed to the teeth, fast, and quiet. This class should be able to unleash insane amounts of firepower and still be fast, quiet, and have good aim. Each member should be able to kill a lot of people within a very short time. This is a lot like night team and super soldier combined. Each member should carry a Firestorm, V2 pistol, Ultrawide, Stealth Atomizer, Walkie Talkie, Binoculars ,camo or black clothing ,tree stands, flat soled shoes (for quietness), and something that makes noise and can be used as a distraction. This class is very effective and hard to beat. I use all these techniques and no one has been able to beat me. Personal Soldier top Credit : Bond Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Dual Firestorms Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : The Base Commanders Personal Soldier is heavily armed, and used for sending valuable messages to other players, and other valuable and dangerous missions. Psycho top Credit : Nitro Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Any Accessories : Any Info : The Psycho adds thrill to the game. There is only one, but with any weapons the Psycho is very dangerous. The Psycho just wanders around and kills anything that moves. Ranger top Credit : Chuck Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm, or 2 V2 Pistols Accessories : Cammo and backpack Info : Rangers are stealth attack troopers and their sole mission is to sneak around and reek havoc on enemies. They sneak undercover and unleash volleys of weapons fire. They are a must have for any army. Reaper Squad top Credit : Chuck Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 1 Firestorm per member, 2 V2 Pistols per member. Accessories : Walkie Talkies , Backpacks, Ultrawides Info : Reaper Squads provide support for infantry in there mixing it up. If you have a Reaper Squad, then one member of each squad should have a walkie talkie. All members of the Reaper Squad have walkie talkies. The squad should be between 3 and 7 members, but depending on the size of the force, it could be larger, or smaller. When called upon, Reaper Squads unleash insane amounts of fire-power upon enemies. A squad will call for help, and the Reapers will unleash upon them with their 1000 ft range. They can also use their pistols to nail things at close range. They can carry as many Ultrawides in their back packs as they want, and by concentrating fire with them, can give close support for infantry and utterly obliterate enemies. Recon top Credit : Deacon Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Any Accessories : Anything needed Info : This member is the brains of the operation. They must find all enemy locations, and create the strategy used for terminating all opposing members. Royal Bomb Squad top Credit : Insomniac Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Ultrawides Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : I made this class because my team specializes too much on night combat making us lose during the day. These people will load their ultrawides with Max blast and fire all at once or in shifts so one can always fire even with the 5 second delay. One member should have double pistols or stealth atomizer so they still can be fast. The theory is that if you fire at everything you can't miss. Scout top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : None Info : The Scout patrols the base perimeter. Sometimes the Scout will go into the enemy's territory or just wonder around looking for trouble. Seal Team Six top Credit : Oddjob Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Assorted Silenced Weapons and Firestorm for Backup Accessories : Camo Outfit/Black Outfit, Walkie Talkies, Hand-Cuffs Info : Should consist of 4-6 members. Excellent marksmen. Excellent stamina. Excellent Soldier to say the least. Good luck finding these guys, they also should be trained in Covert Ops. Move in a staggered snake formation. Use hand signals mostly, but use walkie-talkie to keep in touch with command. Basically, these are the spec ops guys. Give them the hardest mission. Oh, and about the handcuffs.....Prisoners can give out some useful info if properly persuaded. Secret Commander top Credit : Ryan Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : None Info : When your base is under heavy attack, have the Commander hide. Then have this person act like the commander, but have a pistol with a max blast ready. When some body comes into your base, have the fake Commander hit them with the max blast. After the enemy takes the hit, have your support soldiers come in to finish them off. Sharp-Shooter top Credit : Deku Mask Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Dual V2 Pistols Accessories : None Info : The Sharp-Shooter holds both V2 pistols in his/her hands during the game and shoots just about anything that moves. The Sharp-Shooter should be an expert at aim, accuracy, and above all, holding their ground. Also, the S-S shouldn’t need cover as he/she dishes out laser rounds to the enemy. Sniper top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm (silent mode) Accessories : None Info : Armed with the longest range weapon made so far, the Sniper's job is to shoot from as far away as possible. They should be good shots and a master of stealth. Here is a great Instruction Guide made by Nic Bertino . Sniper Commander top Credit : Willberforce Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm and V2 Pistol Accessories : Walkie Talkie, Penknife, Mini Torch Info : The Sniper Commander is the commander of the team, yet he/she has to snipe in stealth, meaning he/she has to move around a lot. He/she has contact with all team members. He/she is a vital member of the team. Sniper Director top Credit : Insomniac Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm and V2 Pistol Accessories : Walkie Talkies, Ghille suit, good spot Info : This class was meant to be a sniper but also watch the enemy and to direct the Nocturnal super soldier, Expendable squads, and power squads. Sniper Rendition top Credit : Nic Bertino Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Anything but Radar Accessories : Foot spikes, tree stands, camo, really good stealth clothes. Info : Just to let everyone know, a sniper CANNOT use a radar. The shape of the gun does not fit the need. No stock and if you wanted to aim the best you would need to put a laser on there but then you lose stealth. So use any other gun (also excluding the laser challenge 2000 sets) like a Firestorm or a Ultrawide. Only the best know the best. Sniper Squad top Credit : Chuck Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm, 1 per member, and Commander may take 1 Pistol also. Accessories : Any stealthy clothes, Commander may take a remote control. Info : This squad consists between 2-8 members. They are armed with Firestorms and wear stealthy clothes. I for example, have 3-D Cammo (I am quite a deadly sharpshooter, and am a direct descendant of any Oakley), and the squad may take a Commander when they have 3 or more members. Soldier top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Ultrawide Accessories : None Info : What would a battle be without Soldiers. These are your basic attack units, armed with the Ultrawide these soldiers are ready to take on the enemy base any time. Spy top Credit : 20belowzero Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Ultrawide Accessories : Walkie Talkie and Binoculars Info : The Spy will sneak behind an enemy, but won't shoot. He/she can radio his teammates and tell where is the opponents are. The Spy works well with Assassins. Stealth Ninja top Credit : Lokie Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm Accessories : Pure Black Outfit Info : The Ninja never gets spotted and wears all black. He/She stays in shadows and snipes from a long range. Super Soldier top Credit : Nitro Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 V2 Pistols, 2 Ultrawides, 2 Firestorms Accessories : None Info : The Super Soldier has to be armed to the teeth. One Super Soldier should easily be able to wipe out small groups of enemies. Super Soldiers do NOT travel in groups, they go solo so they can work in stealth. If a base is under attack, just send in one or two Super Soldiers and the attack should be over in about thirty seconds. If you're prepared to put your life on the line for your base and your commander, and you have a lot of courage, this job is for you! Support Troops top Credit : Chuck Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 Ultrawides and 2 Firestorms Accessories : Back-Pack (if needed) Info : These get in the with their dual weapons and strike at key groups and personnel, and boy, do they get the job done. Suicide Soldier top Credit : PeeWee Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 Ultrawide Blasters Accessories : None Info : This soldier should run into the enemy base and use the Ultrawides to get as many enemies as possible. This can do considerable damage. Super Sniper top Credit : Steve Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm with mods (explained below) Accessories : Stealthy Clothes (Black) Info : A Super Sniper needs a lot of stealth, and a lot of speed to move from place to place. His/her Firestorm should have a centered laser pointer (for laser sight) a night vision scope (night vision is hard to get but my friend has all this stuff and more on a EX-D Super Laser). You also need to have the light internally dimmed or you need to cut the wire to make no light. Really, that's what a good sniper is. Oh yeah, also, a Super Sniper can observe the area around him/her and find a good, safe hiding spot to snipe in. Team Pet top Credit : Nitro Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : None Info : If you're the Team Pet your mission is simple, agree with your team even if they are wrong. This will boost team moral, and moral is very important in combat. Some good things to say to the troops are "That's a great plan!" or "Go fight so you will become victorious!" or maybe "After you die, we'll send in some more troops to take your place!" Just kidding about that last one. Terminator top Credit : Jeff Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm, 2 V2 Pistols, Ultrawide Accessories : Sunglasses, Walkie Talkie, Penknife, Mini Torch Info : The object of the Terminator is to go out first and find where the enemy is and frighten the crap out of them with all his weapons. He/she then warns the rest of his team where they are and he/she starts to kill them. Trickster top Credit : The OrcSlayer Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : 2 V2 Pistols Accessories : Alarm Clock Info : The Trickster works alone and his/her technique is best suited for indoor play on a rainy day ^_^. He/she finds a good hiding spot to shoot from behind, and then sets the alarm clock to go off in a minute or so. This should create a distraction and others will come to investigate and give you easy picking. Turret Operator top Credit : Steve Walsh Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Firestorm w/ Tripod Accessories : None Info : The Turret Operator operates a Firestorm by the base for extra protection. They can not move the turret outside of the base, but they can move it around in the base. Urban Shock Trooper (UST) top Credit : Rommel Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Ultrawide Accessories : Flashlight, Throw able Noisemakers (Optional), and a Laser Pen Info : The Urban Shock Trooper evolved during the violent 'Tractor Shed Wars' on Burbrink 4, an agricultural planet always finding combat within its small, tangled urban centers. Soon after it was found that Firestorms or Pistols simply could not take the Tractor Shed, a young recruit stepped forward. Using only his customized Blinding Ultrawide, he went in alone, and quickly had half the Tractor Shed, including Combine Hill, to himself. His art was reformed afterward in the Hayloft Incident, in which teams had to assault up narrow stairs and plow into hay bale fortifications. The low cost of the soldier's equipment let these to be produced en mass, enabling solid assaults using massive waves. (Pros) Cheap, one weapon, flashlight blinds and intimidates, light also serves as a sight (if you lit it, you hit it). (Cons) Bad outdoors, flashlight may give you away. V2 Slaughters top Credit : Soldier Slaughter Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Any guns he/she can get Accessories : Back Pack Info : Ready for anything. Has to be very accurate. Weapons Handler top Credit : Nitro Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest Weapon(s) : Any guns he/she can get Accessories : Back Pack Info : If anyone has lost their weapons, or had them stolen by the opposing team, the Weapons Handler rushes out to re-arm them. The Back Pack is to store more weapons.

title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge V2 ELS Classes Page
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge V2 ELS Classes Page Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Challenge Classes > Laser Challenge V2 ELS Classes Links: Laser Challenge V2 ELS Classes Base Commander Captain Commander Demolition Soldier Heavy Weapons Soldier Infiltrator/Spy Scout Sniper Soldier Spy Guard Super Weapons Soldier Laser Challenge V2 ELS Classes These classes use the V2 ELS system. Basically it's two teams, red/blue, battling against each other. You can only use ELS gear for these classes. Base Commander top Credit : Bryan Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : ELS Unit, Walkie Talkie Info : This class uses his/her ElS to give teammates needed reloads, and relay info to other players. Captain top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : Ultrawide Rifle Accessories : None Info : He should be a good back-up for the Commander. Commander top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : Firestorm Accessories : None Info : The Commander is the heart of the team. He figures out how to win. He can also come up with some good strategies while under pressure. Demolition Soldier top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : Ultrawide Accessories : None Info : Unlike the Demolition Soldiers using the other gear, he/she uses an Ultrawide. He/she should be an expert at the Ultrawide. You can tell, because they can get it to rapid fire while the gun is in Ultrawide mode (yes it is possible). He/she keeps the enemy base under fire causing havoc. Heavy Weapons Soldier top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : Firestorm Accessories : None Info : A Heavy weapons soldiers job is to back up other soldiers. Using his/her precision aiming ability, they can take down anyone at a long distance. Infiltrator/Spy top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : Walkie Talkie Info : Armed with only a pistol the Infiltrator/Spy is to keep a strong eye on the enemy's base. He/she reports to the Commander. Scout top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : None Info : The Scout patrols the base perimeter. Sometimes the Scout will go into the enemy's or just wonder around looking for trouble. Sniper top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : Firestorm Accessories : None Info : Armed with the longest range weapon made so far, the Sniper's job is to shoot from as far away as possible. They should be good shots and a master of stealth. They have the gun sounds off. Here is a great Instruction Guide made by Nic Bertino . Soldier top Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : Ultrawide Accessories : None Info : Basic Laser Challenge class. The Soldier is an all around unit. Can be given a specific job if necessary, but that's what the other classes are for. Spy Guard top Credit : Brian Caslis Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : V2 Pistol Accessories : None Info : This class simply guards the Spy. Super Weapons Soldier top Credit : Bryan/Warrior Equipment : V2 Front and Back Vest, Red/Blue ELS Adapter Weapon(s) : 2 Firestorms Accessories : ELS Unit, Walkie Talkie Info : This is a Soldier who carries the most powerful weapons, Firestorms. The Captain or Commander sends him to do the dirty work, and take out all the players still alive.

title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Pro Codes
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Pro Codes Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > Laser Challenge Gear > Laser Challenge Pro > Laser Challenge Pro Codes Links: Laser Challenge Pro Gear Overview Startup Codes Power up Codes Overview The Laser Challenge Pro vests were the first Laser Challenge gear to feature code inputs on the gun to activate special abilities. 1 digit and 2 digit codes are used to enter special game modes, while the 3 digit ones are used to activate special abilities during any game mode. The only buttons on the gun are 1, 2, 3, and e. You usually enter a given code and hit e to complete it. If you enter more then 3 digits it just takes the last 3 you entered and uses them. Below is a brief list of all the codes, but Fath0m's page has a more detailed list of them, and tips to extend some features.
