Max 3 hits in rapid succession) 2+2+1+e Enemy Loses All Loaded Ammo 2+2+2+e Hit Another Player, Their Next Shot Will Subtract A Hit From Whoever They Hit 2+2+3+e Transfer A Shield 2+3+1+e Transfer A Max Blast 2+3+2+e Transfer A Reload 2+3+3+e Transfer A Remove A Hit 3+1+1+e Super Shield (10 sec.) 3+1+2+e Hit Remover 3+1+3+e More Time To Escape The Next Time You're Hit 3+2+1+e Next 3 Hits Will Register As 1 3+2+2+e When You're Out Of Reloads, Use This To Get One Last Reload 3+2+3+e Auto-Fire (6 sec.) 3+3+1+e Pause Code, Pull Trigger To Turn Off 3+3+2+e Pause Code, Turns Off In A Few Seconds 3+3+3+e Pause Code, Retype To Turn Off

title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Gear
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > Laser Challenge Gear Links: Laser Challenge Gear Overview Laser Challenge Original Laser Challenge Pro Laser Challenge V2 Laser Challenge Extreme Overview Laser Challenge is currently the most popular laser tag brand. Some laser tag gear produced by them has been questionable, but there have been some great ones. Each "series" listed below has differences in it that sets them apart. Ironically, the Original category will probably contain the oldest, and the newest gear. Laser Challenge Original top Released - 1996-2003 The first of the LC line, the original gear is the second most used, due to it's cheapness and to the fact it can be more easily found then all the other kinds. On this page, I have all the oddball gear Toymax came out with including remakes of the old gear. Laser Challenge Original and Other Gear Laser Challenge Pro top Released - 1997-1998 Toymax tried an attempt at making commercial arena gear. At first it worked, but all of mine, within two months started failing. I have done all the repairs I could, but there are still some occasional problems. Considering I bought this gear fresh of the shelf when it came out ($120 for a twin pack) it was not worth the money, and even for $10 a twin pack wouldn't be. This gear shouldn't be invested in. Laser Challenge Pro Gear Laser Challenge V2 top Released - 1998 This by far has to be the best "series" made by Toymax. I currently use this equipment for my league, and have found it to be quite reliable. Except for some small problems, this gear handles all sorts of weather/conditions. Alas, it is no longer produced, and can be hard to come by, most of the time being sold for more then it originally sold for. I knew I should have stocked up when TRU put it on clearance. Laser Challenge V2 Gear Laser Challenge Extreme top Released - 1999 This category includes both Radar Extreme and Gotcha Extreme. While Radar is much better then Gotcha, they both include the same features which make them like brother and sister (the brother being the better one ;) ). This gear uses SEGA Lock-On type technology. Laser Challenge Extreme Gear

title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Extreme Gear
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Extreme Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > Laser Challenge Gear > Laser Challenge Extreme Gear Links: Laser Challenge Extreme Gear Overview Gear Gotcha Extreme Mini Mayhem Gotcha Extreme Pistol Gotcha Extreme Vest Radar Extreme Pistol Radar Extreme Vest Team Force 250 Sets Gotcha Extreme Set Radar Extreme Set Team Force 250 Set Overview These two gears both have the option of using Team features or the regular features. The Gotcha gear is a more simple version of the Radar gear. Gear top The gear listed is good for expanding the V2 arsenal. This gear isn't great by itself. Gotcha Extreme Mini Mayhem top Released - 2001 Model Number : 80646T/1 Battery Req. : 3 AAA's Range : 150 ft. Ammo : 25 Original Price : ? Info : This gun/vest set is unique in that the vest can actually clip to the gun. This makes it similar to Tiger Toy's Lazer Tag gear. Mini Mayhem still maintains all the features that the Extreme gear has. I would probably buy one of these sets before a Gotcha set :) Gotcha Extreme Mini Mayhem Boxes laserchallenge_extreme_minimayhem.pdf (125 KB) Gotcha Extreme Pistol top Released - 2000 Battery Req. : 3 AA's Range : 350 ft. Ammo : 25 Set(s) : Gotcha Extreme Set Info : This gun is unique in that you can only reload when you are out of ammo. To reload you just tip the gun to the ground and pull the trigger. A very neat idea, if it was used in better equipment. The gun is also single fire only. Gotcha Extreme Pistol (Red) laserchallenge_extreme_gotcha.pdf (1694 KB) Gotcha Extreme Vest top Released - 2000 Battery Req. : --Needs to be plugged into gun-- Accessory Port(s) : 1 Set(s) : Gotcha Extreme Set Info : The vest will only work if it is plugged into the gun. There is no option for the back vest, and when you plug the gun in it enables Max Blasts for the gun. There is team A/B options for the vest and gun too. Gotcha Extreme Vest (Red) laserchallenge_extreme_gotcha.pdf (1694 KB) Radar Extreme Pistol top Released - 1999 Battery Req. : 2 AA's Range : 350 ft. Ammo : 25 Set(s) : Radar Extreme Set Info : These guns are nice add-on's for V2 gear, but if you only play with Radar Extreme gear, you can use their team features. If you want to play on team A, you hold down the Arm button and turn it on, for team B, you hold down Reset button. If you play in team mode, your gun emits a constant IR that lets your opponent know if you have them targeted. It doesn't let you know you have a lock on them unless you can hear their vest. There is also no front light on this gun. Radar Extreme Blue Pistol Radar Extreme Red Pistol laserchallenge_extreme_radar.pdf (1424 KB) Radar Extreme Vest top Released - 1999 Battery Req. : 3 AA's Accessory Port(s) : 1 Set(s) : Radar Extreme Set Info : These sets are basically the same as the V2 vests , but they have the team ability like the Radar Pistols. The Blast Shield button is actually the blue/red circle right on the front, which is sometimes a problem if you lay down you activate your shield. The team A option is enabled by holding in the Blast Shield and turning it on, and the team B option is enabled by holding in the button on the back of the vest. The accessory port on this vest can use an Original Back Vest . This vest also takes hits slower then a V2 vest. Radar Extreme Blue Vest Radar Extreme Red Vest laserchallenge_extreme_radar.pdf (1424 KB) Team Force 250 top Released - 2000 Battery Req. : ? Range : 250 ft. Ammo : 25 Set(s) : Team Force 250 Set Info : This Team Force is very interesting. It uses Radar technology, but a V2 gun casing. It also has the Blast Shields and Max Blasts. Team Force 250 Sets top Each Extreme gear only had one set released. Both sets have enough gear for 2 players in it. Gotcha Extreme Set top Released - 2000 Model Number : 80634T/1 Battery Req. : 6 AA's Contents : 2 (1 blue/1 red) Gotcha Extreme Vest s and 2 (1 blue/1 red) Gotcha Extreme Pistol s Original Price : $29.99 Info : This set is really not as good as V2 or Radar gear. If possible buy either of those sets. Gotcha Extreme Box laserchallenge_extreme_gotcha.pdf (1694 KB) Radar Extreme Set top Released - 1999 Model Number : 80632T/1 Battery Req. : 10 AA's Contents : 2 (1 blue/1 red) Radar Extreme Vest s and 2 (1 blue/1 red) Radar Extreme Pistol s Original Price : $34.99 Info : This set is good to add to your V2 arsenal. Has all the features V2 does with some extras you can use with only other Radar gear. Radar Extreme Box laserchallenge_extreme_radar.pdf (1424 KB) Team Force 250 Set top Released - 2000 Battery Req. : ? AA's Contents : 2 Team Force 250 Guns/Vests (1 Red/ 1 Blue) Original Price : $? Info : This is a set similar to the Gotcha Extreme Mini Mayhem . Team Force 250 Box

title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Original Gear
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Original Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > Laser Challenge Gear > Laser Challenge Original Gear Links: Laser Challenge Original Gear Overview Gear Armine and Explodroid Back Vest B.A.R.T. Droid Capture the Flag Cybersplash Pistol Cybersplash Vest EX-D Super Laser Front Vest Holster Laser Alley Laser Pistol Laser Trap Light Warriors Micromax Blaster Shootback B.A.R.T. Special Edition Sets Cybersplash Set Dual Target Set Ex-treme Pak Patrol Set Super Comp Set Special Edition Set Team Force Set Team Force 2 Set Team Force 2000 Set Tournament Set Overview On this page you will find the gear that Toymax first came out with, and everything else they come out with that doesn't fit into it's own category. This gear is classified as mediocre gear. Gear top All the current Original gear is listed here, with the original prices and a picture if available. Some of the gear listed below, is only available through sets, which are listed later on. Airmine & Explodroid top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : --None needed, plugs into Laser Alley-- Original Price : $14.99 each. Info : These expand the Laser Alley and make it a little more difficult. The reward for defeating one of these expansions is watching them blow off their bases and into the air. Airmine Explodroid Back Vest top Released - 1997 Model Number : 80513 Battery Req. : --None needed, plugs into front vest-- Original Price : $14.99 Set(s) : Super Comp Set Info : If you've got the money and the front vest , then get this! It really cuts down on cheating and makes the game harder, but more fun to play. Don't throw this away either! The Radar Extreme gear currently doesn't have a back vest, and this works with it. Back Vest laserchallenge_original_backvest.pdf (821 KB) B.A.R.T. Droid top Released - 1997 Model Number : 80508 Battery Req. : 3 AA's, 1 9-Volt Original Price : $? and 30 Laser Points Set(s) : Dual Target Set and Tournament Set Info : This little roaming target will help you improve your aim. It should be used on smooth floors. B.A.R.T. B.A.R.T. Box Shootback B.A.R.T. and B.A.R.T. laserchallenge_original_bart.pdf (730 KB) Capture The Flag top Released - 1998 Model Number : 80519 Battery Req. : 2 AA's per flag Original Price : $34.99 Info : There are two timed modes, 30 seconds and 60 seconds. You can also not worry about the sounds and lights (indication of how long you have left to get the flag to the opposite base) and leave the batteries out. Capture the Flag laserchallenge_original_ctf.pdf (798 KB) Cybersplash Pistol top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 2 AA's Range : 150 ft. Ammo : ? Set(s) : Cybersplash Set Info : Just a pistol that has a Cybersplash design. Cybersplash Pistol Cybersplash Vest top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 3 AA's and 1 9-Volt Set(s) : Cybersplash Set Info : The Cybersplash vest has an attached back vest. Cybersplash Vest EX-D Super Laser top Released - 1998 Model Number : 80514 Battery Req. : 4 AA's Range : 200 ft. Ammo : 25 Original Price : $19.99 Set(s) : Ex-treme Pak and Super Comp Set Info : The Extreme Distance (EX-D) Laser Blaster is a large improvement over the Laser Pistols , but it still has that annoying beep. The beep's on the EX-D's are longer and softer then the pistols, but are still there. The EX-D also has rapid fire, which the pistol does not have. EX-D Super Laser laserchallenge_original_exd.pdf (768 KB) Front Vest top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : 3 AA's Accessory Port(s) : 1 Set(s) : Dual Target Set , Ex-treme Pak , Patrol Set , Super Comp Set , and Team Force Set Info : The original Laser Challenge front vest is one of the most common pieces of Laser Challenge gear. A back vest can be plugged into it to add a new challenge to the game. The front vest receives hits slowly, and has a sensor adjuster on the front to increase/decrease sensitivity in bright light. Front Vest Powerup (44 KB) laserchallenge_original_dualtarget.pdf (1531 KB) Holster top Released - 1998 Original Price : 10 Laser Points Info : This comes in handy for some of the smaller Laser Challenge guns. You attach it to the gun, then just clip it to your pants. This allows you to use both hands if necessary. I would not suggest running while it is clipped though. Holster Laser Alley top Released - 1997 Model Number : 80526 Battery Req. : 4 AA's Range : 50 ft. Accessory Port(s) : 2 Original Price : $34.99 Info : The Laser Alley allows you to play 5 different games with 20 different skill levels. You can also plug in Armine and Explodroid to increase the difficulty. Laser Alley Laser Alley Box laserchallenge_original_laseralley.pdf (79 KB) Laser Pistol top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : 2 AA's Range : 50 ft. Ammo : 6 Set(s) : Dual Target Set , Patrol Set , Team Force Set , and Tournament Set Info : This is the original pistol, which doesn't fire very far and has a very annoying beep every 2 or 3 seconds. It has the standard reset button so you don't have to turn the vest on and off when you want to play again. Laser Pistol laserchallenge_original_dualtarget.pdf (1531 KB) Laser Trap top Released - 1997 Model Number : 80511T/2 Battery Req. : 2 AA's Range : 50 ft. (360 o blast) Original Price : $14.99 Set(s) : Ex-treme Pak Info : The Laser Trap has a 5 or 30 second delay mode. While it counts down it emits a buzzing sound. This is great for tossing at someone behind a structure or tree. This has a habit of resetting Laser Challenge gear that is more advanced then the original. This is best used with gear listed only on this page. Laser Trap laserchallenge_original_lasertrap.pdf (64 KB) Light Warriors top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 3 AA's Range : 6 ft. Original Price : $19.99 Info : An interesting Laser Challenge toy. You fire at each others robot trying to deactivate it. When you deactivate your opponents, it's head pops up and spins in circles. Light Warriors Micromax Blaster top Released - 1998 Model Number : 80543 Battery Req. : 5 AAA's; 2 in the gun, 3 in the base. Range : 50 - 150 ft. Ammo : 12 Original Price : $19.99 / 2 units Info : Small and compact, the vest and gun are small enough to fit in your pocket. The holster for the gun actually becomes a clip on vest. There is also a small extender that can boost the distance to about 150 ft. The nice thing about these guns also, they have a sound on/off just like the vest. So you can keep it quiet if you have to. To reload these guns all you have to do is hold the trigger in for about 2 seconds. Micromax Blasters laserchallenge_original_micromax.pdf (1100 KB) Shootback B.A.R.T. top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 3 AA's, 1 9-Volt Range : 25 ft. Original Price : $19.99 Info : He's back with a vengeance. BART has been upgraded to retaliate at all the people shooting at it's brother. This one also takes 10 hits to disable, but fires at you while you try to disable it. Shootback B.A.R.T. Shootback B.A.R.T. with Box Shootback B.A.R.T. and B.A.R.T. Special Edition top Released - 2000 Battery Req. : ? Range : 150 ft. Ammo : ? Set(s) : Special Edition Set Info : This vest/gun combo seems to have a look similar to V2 gear , but it is just like the normal Team Set, just a different look. I'm not sure if it has any special features like Max Blast or Blast Shields. Special Edition Sets top Some of the above gear is only available in sets or is cheaper when offered in a set. Below are all the sets I have seen for the above gear. Cybersplash Set top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 10 AA's and 2 9-Volts Contents : 1 Cybersplash Vest and 1 Cybersplash Pistol Original Price : $34.99 Info : This vest is good for hot summer days. Each time you hit the opponents vest they are squirted with water. The sounds are low and hard to hear sometimes. Cybersplash Box Cybersplash Equipment Cybersplash Gear Dual Target Set top Released - 1997 Model Number : 80503 Battery Req. : 8 AA's, 1 9-Volt Contents : 1 Front Vest , 1 Laser Pistol , and 1 B.A.R.T. Original Price : $? Info : This set is similar to the Patrol Set, but offers a B.A.R.T. to entertain yourself if you have no one to play with. Dual Target Set Box laserchallenge_original_dualtarget.pdf (1531 KB) Ex-treme Pak top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : 16 AA's Contents : 2 Front Vest s, 2 EX-D Rifles, and 1 Laser Trap Original Price : $? Info : This is an interesting set. Good for adding more rifles to your Original gear arsenal. Ex-treme Pak Box Ex-treme Pak Gear Patrol Set top Released - 1997 Model Number : 80501 Battery Req. : 5 AA's Contents : 1 Front Vest and 1 Laser Pistol Original Price : $19.99 Info : This was the first Laser Challenge product I bought. It was one of the first products released by Toymax along with the Team Force set. Special Edition Set top Released - 2000 Battery Req. : ? Contents : 2 Vest/Gun Combo Units Original Price : $? Info : Bascially another version of the original gear with a new look. Special Edition Set Box Super Comp Set top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : 7 AA's Contents : 1 Front Vest , 1 Back Vest , and 1 EX-D Original Price : $29.99 Info : This was a good starter set. If you had to use original gear find this style of gear. Super Comp Set Box Team Force Set top Released - 1997 Model Number : 80504 Battery Req. : 10 AA's Contents : 2 Front Vest s and 2 Laser Pistol s Original Price : $34.99 Info : This set is good for two people just getting into Laser Challenge. Team Force Box Team Force Box with Components Team Force 2 Set top Released - 1999 Battery Req. : 10 AA's Contents : 2 Front Vests and 2 Pistols Original Price : $34.99 Info : This set is more of a cross between Laser Challenge Pro and the Original gear. The guns fire 150 ft. which is longer then the original pistols. The color scheme is also different (yellow, blue, and grey). Team Force 2000 Set top Released - 1999 Battery Req. : 10 AA's Contents : 2 Front Vests and 2 Pistols Original Price : $34.99 Info : Nearly the same as the Team Force 2 set. Team Force 2000 Box Team Force 2000 Gear Tournament Set top Released - 1996 Model Number : 80502 Battery Req. : 5 AA's, 1 9-Volt Contents : 1 Laser Pistol and 1 B.A.R.T. Original Price : $? Info : I think the Dual Target set is better then this.

title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Pro Gear
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge Pro Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > Laser Challenge Gear > Laser Challenge Pro Gear Links: Laser Challenge Pro Gear Overview Laser Challenge Pro Codes Gear Laser Challenge Pro Walkie Talkie Sets Clash Pack Competitor Pack Overview The Laser Challenge Pro gear was an attempt to bring arena style gear to home users. It failed... Laser Challenge Pro Codes top The Laser Challenge Pro vests use 3 digit number codes to activate special abilities. 1 digit and 2 digit codes are used at startup to define game play. 4 digit or higher codes do not exist, but it takes the last 3 you enter and uses that. Laser Challenge Pro Codes Gear top There is not much gear to the Laser Challenge Pro. The gun and vest are connected, and only one accessory was made. Laser Challenge Pro top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : 7 AA's; 4 in front, 3 in back Range : 200 ft. Ammo : Depends on mode Accessory Port(s) : 3 Set(s) : Clash Pack and Competitor Pack Info : This was a very good attempt at arena style gear, but alas it did not work out. The good things about it are that there are 3 sensors! One on the front, one on the back, and one on the gun. Also, there is a little red light on it, clearly visible to everyone else (except in extreme sunlight). It was an indicator to what team you were on (flashing or solid). It has 3 accessory ports which is awesome, but then only one accessory was made for it. The vest also vibrates when hit. Laser Challenge Pro is very faulty. Both of the ones I have purchased developed problems after about 2 months of use. The buttons started to fail or the digital display would stop working. I have done my best to repair them but without tearing the whole circuitry apart, there is no easy way to fix these problems. The Pro vest has a lot of features that lead to the Laser Challenge V2 gear, like Max Blasts, Shields, and playing with or without sound. Laser Challenge Pro Walkie Talkie top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 1 9-Volt Range : 900 ft. Original Price : $24.99 Info : This is the only accessory that was released for the Laser Challenge Pro . You can hear the vest sound effects through them, and is a real nice way to talk to other people playing with Pro vests over great distances. They come in Red or Blue, another way to show what team you are on. Walkie Talkie Sets top Even thought there were only two different sets, a single and a double pack, I will list them. Clash Pak top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : 14 AA's Contents : 2 Laser Challenge Pro Vests Original Price : $119.99 Info : This Pak was cheaper then getting two of the single Pak's. This was not worth the investment due to Pro vests flawed designs. Clash Pak Competitor Pak top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : 7 AA's Contents : 1 Laser Challenge Pro Vest Original Price : $69.99 Info : While really not a set, the Laser Challenge Pro had everything in one bundle, but still failed in the long run. Competitor Pak

title: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge V2 Gear
text: Khan Jal's Laser Challenge V2 Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > Laser Challenge Gear > Laser Challenge V2 Gear Links: Laser Challenge V2 Gear Overview Gear ELS Adapter ELS Computer Firestorm Ultrawide V2 Pistol V2 Vest Sets Adapter Pak ELS Game System Solo Gear Twin Gear Ultrawide Blaster Set Overview Laser Challenge V2 is the best Laser Challenge gear made. It is very durable and has well rounded features. It was also very easy to acquire and didn't cost an arm and a leg. A lot of the V2 gear went on clearance at most Toys R' Us's in 2000. I bought a few sets when they were selling real low. Gear top There are a few options to the V2 line. There are 3 guns, and all vests come with a build in back vest. ELS adapters and computers give some neat features too. All of the guns feature Max Blasts, which is 3 hits for one shot if it hits the opponent, and the ability to turn the gun on with or without sound (first introduced on Micromax Blasters ). All of the vests contain Blast Shields, which are 10 second invulnerability. You start out with 3 Max Blasts and 3 Blast Shields when you activate the Vest and Gun. ELS Adapter top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : --None-- Accessory Port(s) : 2 Set(s) : Adapter Pak and ELS Game System Info : The ELS adapter links the gun to the vest, and enables a new style of playing. ELS Adapters with Box (Blue) ELS Adapters with Box (Red) laserchallenge_v2_elsadapter.pdf (1662 KB) ELS Computer top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 3 AA's Accessory Port(s) : 3; 2 gun and 1 Parallel Set(s) : ELS Game System Info : The ELS computer adds Laser Challenge Pro like features to the V2 line. When you first turn it on you can program the time limit (up to 10 hours), number of hits (up to 30), and how many are on each team (red and blue). Each person plugs in when it asks and everyone gets a unique ID. When the game is over, whether time ran out or one whole team is dead, everyone plugs back in and it pulls up various statistics like hit % for each person, who killed who, the best shooter, and the worst shooter. You cannot shoot your teammates or yourself and you have a limited amount of reloads. You just plug back into the computer to ge t more reloads. The only down side is there can only be a maximum of 8 people. There is also ELS software to connect with a computer and print out the results. ELS Computer ELS Game System Box with Components laserchallenge_v2_elscomputer.pdf (1796 KB) laserchallenge_v2_elsprint.zip (17 KB) Firestorm top Released - 1998 Model Number : 80613 Battery Req. : 6 AA's (take out front 3 to disable recoil) Range : 1000 ft. Ammo : 25 Original Price : $29.99 Info : This is one of the best Laser Challenge guns out there. With an insane range on firing, it takes a while to learn how to aim this beast correctly. It contains a scope that assists you in aiming. Omega was constructed out of a Firestorm. Firestorm laserchallenge_v2_firestorm.pdf (1643 KB) Ultrawide top Released - 1998 Model Number : 80614 Battery Req. : 3 AA's Range : 350 ft./Max spread 50 ft. wide Ammo : 25 Normal/1 shot every 5 seconds on Ultrawide Original Price : $29.99 Set(s) : Ultrawide Blaster Set Info : This is a nice rifle style gun. It has a nice aiming scope and is very easy to aim. You can also activate Ultrawide mode, which spreads the barrels and gives you a small area that you can hit people. This mode is hard to use and normal mode is preferred by most. Ultrawide laserchallenge_v2_ultrawide.pdf (1654 KB) V2 Pistol top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 3 AA's Range : 350 ft Ammo : 25 Set(s) : Solo Gear and Twin Gear Info : This is a very nice pistol. Easy to hold and aim, usually you can fire two of these at once and get some nice hits in. V2 Pistol (Purple) V2 Pistol (Red) laserchallenge_v2_set.pdf (1448 KB) V2 Vest top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 3 AA's Accessory Port(s) : 1 Set(s) : Solo Gear , Twin Gear , and Ultrawide Blaster Set Info : The V2 Vests are nice in that they take hits quickly (10 hits in about 6 seconds) and they have elastic bands to keep them positioned properly. The back vest is hard wired to the front vest, and the port on the front vest is for the ELS system. There is a 9th hit sound which gives you about 3 seconds, and a 10th hit sound which makes it known you are dead. V2 Vest (Purple) V2 Vest (Red) laserchallenge_v2_set.pdf (1448 KB) Sets top V2 had a few sets to choose from. Adapter Pak top Released - 1998 Model Number : 80722 Battery Req. : --None-- Contents : 2 (1 blue/1 red) ELS Adapter s Original Price : $19.99 Info : Adapters were sold in packs, not single. ELS Adapter Pak ELS Adapters with Box (Blue) ELS Adapters with Box (Red) laserchallenge_v2_elsadapter.pdf (1662 KB) ELS Game System top Released - 1998 Model Number : 80711 Battery Req. : 3 AA's Contents : 1 ELS Computer and 2 ELS Adapter s(1 Blue and 1 Red) Original Price : $49.99 Info : This set is the basic begining to the V2 ELS System. ELS Game System Box with Components laserchallenge_v2_elscomputer.pdf (1796KB) Solo Gear top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 6 AA's Contents : 1 V2 Vest and 1 V2 Pistol Original Price : $24.99 Info : The set for one person. laserchallenge_v2_set.pdf (1448 KB) Twin Gear top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 12 AA's Contents : 2 V2 Vest s and 2 V2 Pistol s Original Price : $49.99 Info : I think this set was more common then the Solo Gear. Twin Gear Box Twin Gear Purple Box and Components Twin Gear Red Box and Components laserchallenge_v2_set.pdf (1448 KB) Ultrawide Blaster Set top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : --None-- Contents : 1 V2 Vest and 1 Ultrawide Original Price : $24.99 Info : This is a good set if you want to use a rifle instead of a pistol. Ultrawide Blaster Set Box and Components

title: Khan Jal's Laser Command Gear
text: Khan Jal's Laser Command Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > Laser Command Gear Links: Laser Command Gear Overview Gear Laser Command Hat Laser Command/Attack Pistol Laser Command/Attack Vest Laser Command Vest - Advanced Laser Command Stealth Atomizer Sets Laser Attack Set Laser Command Set Overview Laser Command and Laser Attack are the same exact gear. It seems Astronomical Toys just tried to release it under another name. This gear was pretty cheap and due to it's compatibility with Laser Challenge it was in a pretty good demand. Gear top There were two different vests released, and two different types of guns. Unlike the Original Laser Challenge gear, the vests instead of the guns beeped. Laser Command Hat top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : --None-- Original Price : $? Info : Neat idea, I want a Laser Challenge hat though :). Black Hat Cammo Hat Laser Command/Attack Pistol top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : 2 AA's Range : 100 ft. Ammo : 5 Set(s) : Laser Attack Set and Laser Command Set Info : This is a nice short range gun. Laser Command Pistol Laser Command/Attack Vest top Released - 1997 Battery Req. : ? Set(s) : Laser Attack Set and Laser Command Set Info : This vest takes hits about the same speed as a V2 Vest , but doesn't allow a sensor to added. You can hardwire a Laser Challenge Back Vest to it if you have the knowledge. Laser Command Vest - Advanced top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : ? Info : This vest comes with a back vest. A very wise idea. Laser Command Advanced Vest (Black) Laser Command Advanced Vest (Cammo) Laser Command Stealth Atomizer top Released - 1998 Battery Req. : 2 AA's Range : 200 ft. Ammo : 5 Semi-Automatic/15 Full Automatic Original Price : $12.99 Info : I personally have one of these and I love it. It has a very nice rifle feel, and has single/rapid fire options. title: Khan Jal's Laser Tag Gear
text: Khan Jal's Laser Tag Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear Links: Laser Tag Gear Overview Laser Challenge Laser Command/Laser Attack Lazer Tag Quick Shot SEGA Lock-On Voice Command Lock-On Other Avenger BraveStarr Captain Power Cybershot Electronic Survivor Shot Intruder Laser Combat Laser Pro 9000 MILES Gear Phaser Force Photon Star Bird Zillion Overview Laser Tag type equipment was in use as early as the 70's, but did not make commercial use to early 80's. On this page I list all the known type of Laser Tag equipment. I also have links to specific gear who's popularity has provided me with enough info to bring to you. This page mainly contains home based Laser Tag gear (HBLT) but does include some Arena gear. Some of the gear has a PDF file link which further describes the gear officially. Usually these are instruction manuals. If you don't see a link then I don't have a manual for that gear. If you happen to have that manual and the time, please scan it in (jpeg or pdf) and send it to manual@khanjal.com . It is greatly appreciated and I will put up the link as soon as possible. Also, to read the manuals online, you'll need Adobe Acrobat Reader . Laser Challenge top Manufacturer - Toymax Laser Challenge is by far the most popular of the new age Laser Tag gear. It has several subset versions, each compatible to a degree. Laser Challenge Gear Laser Command/Laser Attack top Manufacturer - Astronomical Toys This is a short lived Laser Tag system that was made in the late 90's. It was fully compatible with Laser Challenge, which helped it grow somewhat popular. I myself only have the rifle from this gear. They also seem to have released Laser Attack, which is identical to Laser Command. Laser Command Gear Lazer Tag top Manufacturer - Tiger Toys and Worlds of Wonder This has to be the main competitor with Laser Challenge. This gear was originally made in the early 80's, but Tiger Toys reused the name for their current gear. Quick Shot top Manufacturer - Radio Shack This is a one time release gear. Not bad from what I was able to see. Quick Shot SEGA Lock-On top Manufacturer - SEGA This Laser Tag system has a lot of features, including a hit counter, and a lock on beam. SEGA Lock-On Voice Command Lock-On top Manufacturer - Playmates Toys This is like the SEGA Lock-On gear, but has a voice command feature to change modes. Voice Command Lock-On Other Gear top This is a list of all the other Laser Tag gear I have been able to discover. Not much is known on these though. Avenger Manufacturer : Milton Bradley Description : No info yet. BraveStarr Manufacturer : Mattel Description : No info yet. Captain Power Manufacturer : Mattel Description : No info yet. Cybershot Manufacturer : ? Description : No info yet. Electronic Survivor Shot Manufacturer : Tomy Description : ESS has a head mounted sensor, and has a gun with single/double shot. More info in a few. Intruder Manufacturer : Milton Bradley Description : No info yet. Laser Combat Manufacturer : ? Description : No info yet. Laser Pro 9000 Manufacturer : Galoob Description : No info yet. MILES Gear Manufacturer : U.S. Military Description : No info yet. Phaser Force Manufacturer : Buddy-L Description : No info yet. Photon Manufacturer : LJN/Entertech Description : Popular Arena Gear. StarBird Manufacturer : Milton Bradley Description : No info yet. Zillion Manufacturer : Sega (Japan) Description : No info yet.

title: Khan Jal's Quick Shot Gear
text: Khan Jal's Quick Shot Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > Quick Shot Gear Links: Quick Shot Gear Overview Gear Electronic Target Lazer Pistol Sensor Set Quick Shot Set Overview Luckily I have a friend who has a set of this. I have gathered all the information I could from it. He received it in 1990 as a present, and several pieces were still missing from it. Although I received information that this gear is made by Radio Shack, I found no proof on the gun itself. Gear top All the gear is aquired in buying one set. Lock-On top Released - 1990 Battery Req. : 4 AA's Set(s) : Quick Shot Set Info : This allows the sensor to be plugged into it and it can receive unlimited hits. When it is hit it has a falling bomb effect. It can be strapped to your body or cliped. Looks like you would mainly use this for target practice. Lazer Pistol top Released - 1990 Battery Req. : 4 AA's Range : 100 ft. Ammo : Unlimited Set(s) : Quick Shot Set Info : The pistol comes with a scope and a shoulder rest. The sensor can also be attached to the pistol, probably adding limited hits to it. I was unable to test this with another due to having only one set at my disposal. Sensor top Released - 1990 Battery Req. : --None-- Set(s) : Quick Shot Set Info : The sensor plugs into either the Electronic Target or Lazer Pistol, allowing either to be shot. Sets top Quick Shot was only available in one set. Quick Shot Set top Released - 1990 Model Number : QS2040 Battery Req. : 8 AA's Contents : 1 Lazer Pistol , 1 Electronic Target , and 1 Sensor Original Price : $? Info : This set is the only set that I know for this gear. I wish I had access to two to test them out further. Quick Shot Set Quick Shot Set

title: Khan Jal's SEGA Lock-On Gear
text: Khan Jal's SEGA Lock-On Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > SEGA Lock-On Gear Links: SEGA Lock-On Gear Overview Gear Lock-On Lock-On 2 Overview From what I have read about these sets, they seem to be well designed and fun to play with. They lack the 360 o sensor, but have other features which make up for it. A lot of information was used from Razor Team Headquarters website. Gear top All the gear is linked. There are a few versions of the gear out, but they work together. Lock-On top Released - 1980 Battery Req. : 1 9-Volt and 2 C's Range : 350 ft. Ammo : Single/Rapid fire(5 shots/sec), 10 high powered shots Original Price : $? Info : So far this gear is only available over seas. It has a lot of neat features. The headset has a visor that displays your hit points in a 7 segment LED. The visor can be rotated at will. The two triggers sound neat. The main one is in the usual place. If held, it produces a rapid fire effect (5 shots/sec). The second button is a 3 hit per one shot. It is called the High Power Shot (kind of like the Max Blast button on the Laser Challenge Gear, but you get 10 instead of 3 and you have 6 sec to shoot it before you loose it). The vest has 9 hits instead of 10 like LC. The team switches are neat. You decide between two teams. If you want to be on team A, you set the switch to it and turn on your gun. You do the same for team B. SEGA Lock-On SEGA Lock-On Gun Diagram SEGA Lock-On Head Sensor Diagram Lock-On 2 top Released - 1985 Battery Req. : 4 AA's and 2 C's Range : 350 ft. Ammo : Single/Rapid fire(5 shots/sec), 10 high powered shots Original Price : $? Info : This unit is slightly shorter and a little thicker than the original. The two are fully compatible. The Hi Power Shot is a little bit harder to press. The headset is more compact which makes it sit to one side of your head. A bit ackward because it is thicker and has a more definate square shape to it. SEGA Lock-On 2

title: Khan Jal's Voice Command Lock-On Gear
text: Khan Jal's Voice Command Lock-On Gear Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Tag Gear > Voice Command Lock-On Gear Links: Voice Command Lock-On Gear Overview Gear Voice Command Lock-On Overview Similar to the SEGA Lock-On except this uses voice commands. Gear top This uses voice commands to control the gun. It is also compatible with the other Lock-On gear. Voice Command Lock-On top Released - 1990 Battery Req. : 4 AA's and 2 C's Range : ? Ammo : ? Original Price : $? Info : Not much is known about this Lock-On. It seems to have been released mainly in Japan. I found a small section on a message board posted by Peter : It has 3 modes Voice command Without Voice commands Original The voice command instructions are complicated and it does not work well in reality you can still use the gear without the annoying temperamental voice commands To turn on the gun hold down the display button (square) and the mode button (round) at the same time while turning the gun to either Team A or B The gun will say Full Power (equals 100 hit points) You get 50 shots in blast mode before a 5 second automatic reload cycle starts. During the reload you may not shoot, but you may be tagged. You get 25 shots in hyper mode before reloading Change modes by pressing the mode button In Blast mode you do 6 points each hit In hyper mode you do 12 points each hit to a target in blast mode, but 20 points to a target who is also in hyper mode In other words if all players are in blast mode it takes 17 hits to take out an opponent but with everyone in Hyper mode it only take 5 hits to eliminate a target There is also a destroy mode that becomes available after you get shot down to 40 points or less. It allows you to do 40 points of damage to your target in one shot, but then your gun goes through the 5 second reload feature. Basically it is a desperate feature and not very useful To active the original game features hold down the display button while turning the on switch to team A or B The gun will count up to 9 hit points and count down to 0 with each tag unlimited ammo, no reload cycle the Mode button becomes a "triple shot" button. you get a maximum of 10 triple shots. once you press the button the gun beep quickly for 6 seconds. If the gun is not fired before the 6 seconds is up the grenade is "lost" Oh yeah, the guns beep constantly and can not be stopped. If you hold the trigger down it will be quieted to a "click" sound. You are limited to team A vs. team B games and can not play everyman for himself type games you can not shot your self or team mates the Lock on feature lets you scan the woods and the target gun will beep loudly when you are aiming at it (this would be a good time to pull the trigger, or if you are the target that is beeping, DUCK! Voice Command Lock-On

title: Khan Jal's Laser Commandos Page
text: Khan Jal's Laser Commandos Page Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Commandos Links: Laser Commandos Detailed History Equipment Games Played Members Rules About the Laser Commandos We are a league located in northwest PA. We play whenever we get a chance, and have played against other leagues. We use mainly V2 gear, with some Laser Command gear, Radar Extreme, and Gotcha Extreme. We allow modifications to guns within reason. More info about us can be found below. History of the Laser Commandos It all started out with the fun weapons from Nerf. My sister and I would play that constantly. We eventualy had built up a small arsenal, but started to see some drawbacks. First off, the range of the weapons weren't good enough for us, and second, trying to rescue you're ammo without getting hit was tiresome. We also had the basic problem of loosing ammo, and the ammo getting damaged. So we got bored with Nerf. Then Toymax comes out with a laser tag system called Laser Challenge. This is my first time seeing something like this (that I can remember). Well after some serious persuasion to our dad (didn't like us running with gun type things), we got a set. It was a patrol set for each of us. Then it was downhill from there. Every time Toymax released a new type of gear, we were one of the first to get it. Although we didn't get some gear (like Laser Alley, the pro walkie talkies, and the BARTs) we did get the majority of the lines. When we reached the V2 level, we had some older gear that we used along with it. I tried getting some of my friends to play. Mike R. finally jumped in and started playing, and at first we let him borrow our original gear, but then for Christmas he got his own stuff! For a while it was the three of us, but eventually we got some hardcore players and they come to almost every game. You can see under the members section who plays in our league.

title: Khan Jal's Laser Commandos Equipment Page
text: Khan Jal's Laser Commandos Equipment Page Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Commandos > Laser Commandos Equipment Links: Laser Commandos Equipment Guns Vests My Equipment Guns We use V2 compatible guns mainly. We prefer V2 guns to the small variety at our disposal, for those who like their preferences. Any guns that break are not a big worry. We have enough for two guns to one person. Vests top We use V2 vests for our main choice. Due to the problem if we loose a vest we have one less person that can play, we value the vests more so then the guns. The vests take 10 hits and have 3 5-sec blast shields. My Equipment top I have enough equipment that I can support the league if no one else had any. Thankfully, some people in the league have their on, thus allowing me to use my equipment to supply more people. I will not sell any of my equipment either. title: Khan Jal's Laser Commandos Rules Page
text: Khan Jal's Laser Commandos Rules Page Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Commandos > Laser Commandos Rules Links: Laser Commandos Rules Borders Death Guns - Qualifications New Players Physical Contact Starting a Game Time Outs Treatment - Between Players Treatment - Equipment Vest - Deactivation Vest - Hiding Walkie Talkies About the Laser Commandos Rules Have have instated some rules to keep the game fair and fun. All Laser Commandos members are to abide by the rules at all times. New attendees are given some time to adjust and learn the rules, which are usually told from an experienced member. Below are the list of rules we play by. 1. Borders top While there are no real borders, there may be some off-limit places. Avoid these places, and try not go go too far from the main playing area. 2. Death top When you die, return to the designated death spot (normally the garage or porch) and wait for the game to be over. You cannot talk or interfere with any of the live players, just the other dead ones. It is also good if you have a way to identify the dead players en transit to the death spot (i.e. raised hands). 3. Guns - Qualifications top Laser Challenge guns and Laser Challenge compatible guns are allowed on two conditions: 1) They must have sound in the daytime 2) The must have lights or sound at night. If guns meet these qualifications for the time the game is being played, they are allowed to be used. Modified weapons are allowed as long as they meet those qualifications too. 4. New Players top It is understood that when you first play, you won't be good at it. It normally takes 2 or 3 games to become pretty well versed at it. New players will be assisted by the experienced ones. Each new person will receive a guide. If there happens to be more new people then experts, they will be paired up. The guide is responsible for teaching the new player all the aspects of the game. The guide will help the new player the duration of time they are there. New players will be given reset disabled guns. This will ensure they don't accidentally reset themselves or others. 5. Physical Contact top There will be no physical contact between opposing teams during a game. You cannot knock them over, steal their weapon, or hold them in place. You must stay at least 3 feet from them at all times. 6. Starting a Game top When the game is ready to begin, everyone turns or resets their vest at the same time (or close to the same time) and they have till the blast shield available alert sounds, then the game begins. 7. Time Outs top You can call a time out if you hurt yourself by falling, hitting something, or any other means of injury. If you see someone get injured, you can call a time out. When a time out is called, you stop playing and help the person out. If the person is ok, everyone MUST return to his or her position when the time out was called. If the person is not ok, he/she should be assisted to the dead spot, and checked out. If they really seem in bad condition, medical help should be contacted. 8. Treatment - Between Players top Every person should be respectable to each other, even if there are previous hostilities between players. If I see excessive rudeness or torment toward a player or players, that person will be banned from attending for a period of time or permanently. 9. Treatment - Equipment top Equipment borrowed from me or someone who has extra should be treated as best as possible. It is understood that accidents do happen. This game is about hiding and strategy. Equipment will get nicked and scratched from use. This is ok by me, but LC V2 equipment is becoming rare, and in the future, this might be a problem. 10. Vest - Deactivation top If your vest gets deactivated by being shot 10 times, getting reset, or getting turned off, return to the dead spot. Even if you accidentally reset it, go to the dead spot. No exceptions will be made. 11. Vest - Hiding top You are allowed to hide behind objects to avoid being shot, but if you look around the object, you must expose your vest. This makes the game more challenging that you can get shot by just looking around a corner or tree. You can blindly shoot with your gun around an object, but as soon as you poke you head out, you must expose your vest too. 12. Walkie Talkies top Walkie Talkies are allowed to be used during games. Both teams should have them, if one team doesn't want to use them, they don't have to. The teams must stay on their own channels, and aren't allowed to "tap" into their opponent's channel. To ensure privacy, teams should make up their own code words to use in place of regular ones.

title: Khan Jal's Omega Page
text: Khan Jal's Omega Page W mega Last Updated: Index > Laser Tag > Laser Commandos > Laser Commandos Equipment > Omega Links: Omega Info First Modification Second Modification Third Modification Fourth Modification Fifth Modification Omega Omega is my pride and joy Laser Challenge Gun. I wanted a good weapon, and one that stood out that was the best, so I made Omega. It has taken years to get her to this state, but right now she stands as the most powerful weapon in my arsenal. Info Class Type Modified Firestorm Given Name Omega First Modified August 1999 --Features Added Recoil Maker removed (rewired battery compartment to provide power to another V2 system) 1x scope (drilled out two top screw holes, and secured it by plastic wraps) V2 pistol system (took a pistol apart, cut off and sodered arm, trigger, and on/off switches to new ones, hot glued pistol barrel right above firestorm barrel) Painted Omega Symbol (Model paint and a computer print out off the symbol made into a pattern tracer) Second Modification July 17, 2000 --Features Added 2nd Pistol IR lens (took a nonworking pistol's barrel and added it to the current one) Ultrawide IR lenses added (took both barrels and added them to the current network of IR lenses) Third Modification November 12 - December 21, 2000 --Features Added Repaired Minor Malfunctions Removed all extra barrels and added only the two Pistol barrels, and one Gotcha Barrel Rewired network of extra barrels. Added Laser Pointer Fourth Modification January 26, 2001 --Features Added Hot glued flashlight to gun. Fifth Modification November 17, 2001 --Features Added Repaired and Rewired Omega's malfunctioning parts. Planned Future Modifications Camera Mount (pinhole cam and a wireless transmitter, estimated cost: around $300. It'll be awhile for that one) Gun Stand (to keep the gun from laying on the glued barrels) Omega the First Modification Omega Modification 2 - Front View Omega Modification 2 - Front Left View Omega Modification 2 - Front Right View Omega Modification 2 - Left View Omega Modification 3 - Front View Omega Modification 3 - Angle View Omega Modification 3 - Side View
